title: Random Token Generator
shortDescription: Generate secure random tokens for API keys, reset links and test secrets.
metaDescription: Generate secure random tokens in Base64URL, hex or alphanumeric formats for API keys, reset links and tests.
intro: Create random tokens with a chosen length, format, count and optional prefix.
overview:
This tool creates random token strings for technical workflows where a predictable value would be unsafe or misleading. Choose Base64URL for URL-safe secrets, hex for checksum-like values, or alphanumeric when a system only accepts letters and digits.

Use these tokens for test secrets, reset-link examples, temporary identifiers and development fixtures. Do not paste production secrets into shared tickets or public documents, and store real credentials in a password manager or secret store.
howItWorks: Choose the token length and number of tokens.
Select Base64URL, hex or alphanumeric output.
Add an optional prefix when your system expects one.

examples.title: Hex token with prefix
examples.input: Length: 24; Count: 1; Format: Hex; Prefix: tok_
examples.description: A prefixed hex token for systems that label token types.

examples.title: Multiple test tokens
examples.description: Several short test tokens can be generated at once for mock users.

faq.question: Which token format should I choose?
faq.answer: Use Base64URL for URL-safe secrets, hex for systems that expect hexadecimal values, and alphanumeric for strict forms that reject symbols.

faq.question: Is the prefix counted in the random length?
faq.answer: No. The length setting controls only the random part. The prefix is added before each token after generation.

faq.question: Can I use these tokens in production?
faq.answer: The generator uses browser cryptographic randomness when available, but production credentials should still be created and stored inside your normal secret-management process.

faq.question: Why generate more than one token?
faq.answer: Multiple tokens are useful for fixtures, QA accounts and examples where each record needs a different secret-looking value.
